---
title: "U.S. Labor Market Trends (2020–2023)"
author: "Shahlan Finchan + Group 3"
date: "2026-02-18"
output: html_document
runtime: shiny
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(shiny)

## Read Excel file 
t9_2020 <- read_excel(
  "2020 Table 9.xlsx", 
  col_types = c("text", "text", "text", "text", "text", "text")
)

# 2. Convert numeric columns from text to numbers
t9_2020 <- t9_2020 %>%
  mutate(across(-Occupation, readr::parse_number))

# 3. Pivot longer to create tidy format
t9_tidy <- t9_2020 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Occupation,              
    names_to = c("sex", "age"),       
    names_sep = "_",
    values_to = "employment"         
  ) %>%
  mutate(age = as.numeric(age))       

# 4. Inspect tidy data
glimpse(t9_tidy)


```

```{r} 
# Implementing Shiny with dropdowns
selectInput("sex_input", "Select Sex:", choices = unique(t9_tidy$sex))
selectInput("age_input", "Select Age:", choices = unique(t9_tidy$age))

# Filtered table
renderTable({
  req(input$sex_input, input$age_input)
  t9_tidy %>%
    filter(sex == input$sex_input, age == input$age_input)
})
```

```{r} 
## Practice Histogram  
ggplot(t9_tidy %>% filter(sex != "Total"), 
       aes(x = employment, fill = sex)) +
  geom_histogram(position = "dodge", bins = 20, alpha = 0.8) +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Employment by Sex") +
  xlab("Employment Count") +
  ylab("Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()
```

The data is right-skewed, indicating that most indivisuals fall in the lower employment count brackets.
